Guideline: Use Blade components for reusable UI
Description: Extract repeated markup into named Blade components
Do: Use x-* components with @props for all reusable UI
Don't: Duplicate HTML blocks across views

Description: Define one master layout and extend it per page
Do: @extends layout with named @section blocks
Don't: Duplicate header/footer HTML in every view
Code Bad: Full HTML in every view file

Description: Declare accepted props inside components with @props
Do: @props with defaults to document component API
Don't: Pass arbitrary variables without declaration

Description: Build class strings conditionally without ternary noise
Do: @class directive for conditional class binding
Don't: String concatenation or nested ternaries

Guideline: Use named slots for flexible layouts
Description: Named slots let callers inject content into specific regions
Do: @slot('header') and $slot for flexible component APIs
Don't: Hard-code all sub-sections inside components

Guideline: Use Blade directives instead of raw PHP
Description: Blade directives are readable and IDE-supported
Do: @if @foreach @forelse @empty instead of <?php ?>
Don't: Raw PHP tags inside Blade templates

Description: Use double curly braces for XSS-safe output
Do: {{ }} for all user-supplied or dynamic text
Don't: {!! !!} for untrusted data

Description: Vite integration handles cache busting and HMR automatically
Do: @vite(['resources/css/app.css' 'resources/js/app.js'])
Don't: Manual script/link tags with hardcoded paths

Description: Two-way data binding keeps component state in sync
Do: wire:model for all form inputs managed by Livewire
Don't: Manual JavaScript listeners syncing to component

Description: Validate on input rather than only on submit
Do: wire:model.live + #[Validate] for instant feedback
Don't: Only validate on form submit
Code Bad: <input wire:model="email"> with validate() on submit only

Description: Bind UI events to component methods cleanly
Do: wire:click for buttons and interactive elements
Don't: JavaScript fetch calls replicating Livewire actions

Description: mount() for init; updated() for reactive side effects
Do: mount() for initialization updatedFoo() for property changes
Don't: Heavy logic in render() or __construct()

Guideline: Use lazy loading for heavy components
Description: Defer render of expensive components until visible
Do: wire:init or lazy attribute on components
Don't: Load all Livewire components on page load
Code Bad: <livewire:analytics-chart /> with heavy DB queries on mount

Guideline: Integrate Alpine.js for local UI state
Description: Use Alpine.js for UI-only state that doesn't need server round-trips
Do: x-data / x-show / x-transition for tooltips dropdowns
Don't: Livewire server calls for purely visual toggle state

Description: Always indicate to users when a server action is in progress
Do: wire:loading.attr="disabled" and wire:loading elements
Don't: Provide no feedback while Livewire request is in flight

Description: Livewire's trait manages chunked upload and temp storage
Do: WithFileUploads trait + wire:model for file inputs
Don't: Manual multipart form submissions for Livewire pages

Guideline: Use Inertia page components as route endpoints
Description: Each page is a Vue/React component rendered server-side via Inertia::render()
Do: Inertia::render('Dashboard' ['data' => $data]) in controllers
Don't: Return JSON and fetch from JavaScript

Description: Middleware share() provides auth user and flash to every page
Do: Share auth/flash in HandleInertiaRequests middleware
Don't: Pass auth to every Inertia::render() call

Description: Inertia Link intercepts clicks for SPA-like transitions
Do: <Link href="/dashboard"> instead of <a href>
Don't: Regular <a> tags for internal navigation

Guideline: Use useForm for form state and submission
Description: Inertia's useForm manages progress errors and transforms
Do: useForm for all page-level forms, form.post() for submit
Don't: Axios/fetch for form submissions on Inertia pages

Guideline: Use persistent layouts to preserve state
Description: Wrap pages in a persistent layout so header/sidebar don't remount
Do: layout property on page component for persistent UI
Don't: Re-render full layout on every page visit
Code Bad: No layout — full page reload feel on navigation

Description: Server-side rendering improves SEO and first paint
Do: Enable Inertia SSR for marketing and public pages
Don't: Client-only rendering for all pages including public
Code Bad: No SSR on pages requiring good SEO

Guideline: Set up Tailwind CSS via Vite
Description: Use Vite + tailwindcss plugin for fast HMR and optimized builds
Do: Install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite and configure vite.config.js
Don't: Laravel Mix or manual PostCSS pipeline for new projects
Code Bad: Laravel Mix with require('tailwindcss') in webpack

Guideline: Purge unused styles via content config
Description: Tailwind scans Blade and JS files to tree-shake unused classes
Do: content: ['./resources/views/**/*.blade.php' './resources/js/**/*.{js
Don't: vue}']
Code Good: No content config — ship all 3MB of CSS

Description: class-based dark mode integrates with server-rendered preference
Do: darkMode: 'class' with a toggle that sets class on <html>
Don't: Media query only — no user override possible

Description: Extract only truly repeated multi-class patterns
Do: @apply for BEM base classes shared across many components
Don't: @apply for every single element — defeats Tailwind's purpose

Guideline: Configure custom design tokens in CSS
Description: Define brand colors spacing fonts as CSS variables consumed by Tailwind
Do: Custom @theme tokens matched to brand guidelines
Don't: Magic color hex codes scattered across Blade templates

Guideline: Use anonymous Blade components for UI primitives
Description: Blade files in resources/views/components/ auto-register as x-* components
Do: Anonymous components for buttons alerts badges cards
Don't: Blade @includes for anything reusable

Guideline: Use class-based components for complex logic
Description: PHP class components can inject services and pre-process data
Do: app/View/Components/ class when component needs PHP logic
Don't: Blade @php blocks for business logic inside templates

Description: Pass through HTML attributes like class id aria to root element
Do: $attributes->merge() on root element of components
Don't: Ignore caller-provided HTML attributes silently
Code Bad: <div class="btn"> — drops extra class/id from caller

Description: Keep variant/size/color logic in a PHP class or helper not in Blade
Do: Variant class or match() expression in component class
Don't: Long @if chains for variants inside Blade templates
Code Good: public function classes(): string { return match($this->variant) { 'primary' => 'bg-blue-600', } }

Description: Use {{ $slot ?? '' }} or named slot defaults so components are usable empty
Do: Default content in slots for optional regions
Don't: Require every slot to be filled — throws errors on empty usage

Description: Prefix third-party or module components to avoid collisions
Do: Register custom prefix via Blade::componentNamespace()
Don't: Mix first-party and package component names with no prefix

Description: Move validation rules out of controllers into dedicated FormRequest classes
Do: php artisan make:request and define rules() + authorize()
Don't: Inline validate() in controller actions
Code Good: class StorePostRequest extends FormRequest { public function rules() { return ['title' => 'required|max:255']; } }

Guideline: Preserve old input on validation failure
Description: Use old() to repopulate form fields after server-side error redirect
Do: old('field') as default value on all form inputs
Don't: Empty form fields when validation fails

Description: Use the @error directive for inline field-level error messages
Do: @error('field') to show per-field messages
Don't: Dump $errors->all() in one block at top of form

Guideline: Use CSRF token on all forms
Description: CSRF protection is enabled by default — include @csrf in every form
Do: @csrf in every POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE form
Don't: Disable VerifyCsrfToken middleware for convenience

Description: HTML forms only support GET/POST — use @method for REST actions
Do: @method('PUT') inside form for update/delete routes
Don't: Route::post for all mutations including updates

Description: Flash success/error in controller; read in layout with session()
Do: session('status') in layout for global flash display
Don't: Re-query DB or pass flash from every controller individually

Guideline: Eager load relationships to prevent N+1
Description: Always eager load related models used in views with with()
Do: with() in queries before passing collections to views
Don't: Lazy-load relations inside Blade loops

Description: Use cache() helper to wrap expensive rendered partials
Do: cache() around slow partials that change infrequently
Don't: Re-render identical content on every request

Description: Always paginate collections in list views
Do: ->paginate() or ->simplePaginate() with {{ $items->links() }}
Don't: ->get() for large tables in views

Description: Offload emails notifications and heavy processing to queues
Do: Dispatch jobs for anything taking >200ms
Don't: Block HTTP request with slow operations

Description: Laravel resolves models automatically — avoids manual find()
Do: Type-hint model in controller method
Don't: Manual User::findOrFail($id) in every method
Code Good: public function show(User $user): View { return view('users.show' compact('user')); }

Guideline: Enable HTTP response caching for static content
Description: Cache control headers for pages that rarely change
Do: Cache-Control headers via middleware for public pages
Don't: No caching — serve every response fresh
Severity: No cache headers on marketing pages

Description: {{ }} auto-escapes HTML — never use {!! !!} on user data
Do: {{ }} for all untrusted or dynamic content
Don't: {!! !!} for user-controlled strings

Guideline: Protect routes with Gate and Policy
Description: Use policies for authorization — never inline permission checks in views
Do: @can / Gate::allows() for UI visibility; policy()->authorize() for actions
Don't: Hardcode role checks inline across templates

Description: Check MIME type size and store outside public root
Do: Store in storage/app/private + validate mimes and max
Don't: Store raw upload in public/ without validation
Code Bad: 'avatar' => 'required' with no MIME or size check

Guideline: Use signed URLs for temporary links
Description: Generate expiring URLs for private downloads or email confirmations
Do: URL::signedRoute() or temporarySignedRoute()
Don't: Expose sequential IDs in download URLs without auth
Code Bad: route('file.download' $id) with no expiry or signature

Guideline: Set a strict Content Security Policy
Description: CSP headers prevent XSS injection of external scripts
Do: spatie/laravel-csp or custom middleware to emit CSP header
Don't: No CSP — browser runs any injected script
